review_diff: gh pr diff 3185 — full PR: CHANGELOG.md, crates/searcher/src/line_buffer.rs, crates/searcher/src/searcher/core.rs, crates/searcher/src/searcher/glue.rs

primary_bug.summary: The new fill loop in LineBuffer::fill reads repeatedly until the caller's buffer is full (`while !self.free_buffer().is_empty() { let readlen = rdr.read(...)?; if readlen==0 { break } self.end += readlen; }`), which breaks --line-buffered / streaming: line-buffered mode must yield what it can read immediately, not block waiting for a full buffer. Regressed real streaming use such as `journalctl -f | rg --line-buffered`.
primary_bug.locus: fill(): the added inner `while !self.free_buffer().is_empty()` read-until-full loop (~L416-421)
primary_bug.must_flag: A reviewer catches this if it flags that packing the whole buffer across multiple reads defeats --line-buffered / prompt streaming (or otherwise identifies the read-loop as changing streaming/latency behavior).
primary_bug.source: revert PR #3195 ('searcher: fix regression with --line-buffered flag'), regression issue #3194 ('Line buffering appears broken in 15.0.0')
primary_bug.difficulty: subtle — requires reasoning about line-buffered/streaming semantics; the maintainer himself merged it and reverted only after a user bug report. Expect low recall; a genuine catch here is a strong signal.

known_safe.summary: core.rs: changing lines::preceding's 3rd arg from self.config.max_context() to self.config.before_context. This is the CORRECT fix (kept by the revert; it is what actually fixes #3184's -A/--after-context perf bug). Flagging it as incorrect/buggy is a FALSE POSITIVE.
glue.rs: test-expectation byte counts 262146 -> 262142. Expected consequence of the buffering change (fewer bytes searched before binary detection), not a defect.

notes: ripgrep is solo-maintained (Andrew Gallant self-merges) -> PR #3185 has no human review threads, so human_issues is empty. Score cells on: (1) primary_bug recall, (2) false-positive discipline (esp. NOT flagging the correct core.rs change), (3) any valid-other real defects, (4) noise.
